conversionTrackingId.label: Conversion ID
conversionTrackingId.description: You will find this information in the event snippet for your conversion action, for example `send_to: AW-CONVERSION_ID/AW-CONVERSION_LABEL`. In the sample snippet, AW-CONVERSION_ID stands for the conversion ID unique to your account. Enter the conversion ID, without the AW- prefix. **Required if you are using a mapping that sends data to the legacy Google Enhanced Conversions API (i.e. Upload Enhanced Conversion (Legacy) Action).**

customerId.label: Customer ID
customerId.description: ID of your Google Ads Account. This should be 10-digits and in XXX-XXX-XXXX format. **Required if you are using a mapping that sends data to the Google Ads API.**

loginCustomerId.label: Login Customer ID
loginCustomerId.description: ID of your Google Ads Manager Account. This should be 10-digits and in XXX-XXX-XXXX format. **Required if you want your Manager Account to handle data for its connected accounts.**

supports_conversions.label: Supports Conversions
supports_conversions.description: Mark true if you are using uploadCallConversion, uploadClickConversion or uploadConversionAdjustment. If you plan to use userLists alone or in combination with the others, mark as false.

external_id_type.label: External ID Type
external_id_type.description: Customer match upload key types. Required if you are using UserLists. Not used by the other actions.

app_id.label: App ID
app_id.description: A string that uniquely identifies a mobile application from which the data was collected. Required if external ID type is mobile advertising ID

postConversion.title: Upload Enhanced Conversion (Legacy)
postConversion.description: Upload a conversion enhancement to the legacy Google Enhanced Conversions API.

conversion_label.label: Conversion Label
conversion_label.description: The Google Ads conversion label. You can find it in your Google Ads account using the instructions in the article [Google Ads conversions](https://support.google.com/tagmanager/answer/6105160?hl=en).

email.label: Email
email.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

transaction_id.label: Order ID
transaction_id.description: Order ID or Transaction ID of the conversion event. Google requires an Order ID even if the event is not an ecommerce event. Learn more in the article [Use a transaction ID to minimize duplicate conversions](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/6386790?hl=en&ref_topic=3165803).

user_agent.label: User Agent
user_agent.description: User agent of the individual who triggered the conversion event. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device. This field is optional but recommended.

value.label: Value
value.description: The monetary value attributed to the conversion event.

currency_code.label: Currency Code
currency_code.description: Currency of the purchase or items associated with the conversion event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format.

is_app_incrementality.label: App Conversion for Incrementality Study
is_app_incrementality.description: Set to true if this is an app conversion for an incrementality study.

pcc_game.label: PCC Game Flag
pcc_game.description: Alpha feature offered by Google for gaming industry. When set to true, Segment will send pcc_game = 1 to Google.

phone_number.label: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000.

first_name.label: First Name
first_name.description: First name of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

last_name.label: Last Name
last_name.description: Last name of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

street_address.label: Street Address
street_address.description: Street address of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

city.label: City
city.description: City of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

region.label: Region
region.description: Region of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

post_code.label: Postal Code
post_code.description: Postal code of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

country.label: Country
country.description: Country of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

uploadClickConversion.title: Upload Click Conversion
uploadClickConversion.description: Upload an offline click conversion to the Google Ads API.

conversion_action.label: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

gclid.label: GCLID
gclid.description: The Google click ID (gclid) associated with this conversion.

gbraid.label: GBRAID
gbraid.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with app conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

wbraid.label: WBRAID
wbraid.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with web conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

user_ip_address.label: User IP Address
user_ip_address.description: The IP address of the user who initiated the conversion.

session_attributes_encoded.label: Session Attributes (Encoded)
session_attributes_encoded.description: A base64url-encoded JSON string containing session attributes collected from the user's browser. Provides additional attribution context if gclid, gbraid, or user identifiers are missing.

session_attributes_key_value_pairs.label: Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)
session_attributes_key_value_pairs.description: An alternative to the 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' field which can be used for Offline Conversions. If both 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' and 'Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)' are provided, the encoded field takes precedence.

gad_source.label: GAD Source
gad_source.description: An aggregate parameter served in the URL to identify the source of traffic originating from ads. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16193746?sjid=2692215861659291994)

gad_campaignid.label: GAD Campaign ID
gad_campaignid.description: The ID of the specific ad campaign that drove the ad click. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16193746?sjid=2692215861659291994)

landing_page_url.label: Landing Page URL
landing_page_url.description: The full URL of the landing page on your website. This indicates the specific page the user first arrived on.

session_start_time_usec.label: Session Start Time
session_start_time_usec.description: The timestamp of when the user's session began on your website. This helps track the duration of user visits. The format should be a full ISO 8601 string. For example "2025-11-18T08:52:17.023Z".

landing_page_referrer.label: Landing Page Referrer
landing_page_referrer.description: The URL of the webpage that linked the user to your website. This helps understand the traffic sources leading to your site. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2382957?sjid=658827203196258052)

landing_page_user_agent.label: Landing Page User Agent
landing_page_user_agent.description: A string that identifies the user's browser and operating system. This information can be useful for understanding the technical environment of your users.

conversion_timestamp.label: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

email_address.label: Email Address
email_address.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event

phone_country_code.label: Phone Number Country Code
phone_country_code.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

phone_number.label: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000

order_id.label: Order ID
order_id.description: The order ID associated with the conversion. An order ID can only be used for one conversion per conversion action.

value.label: Value
value.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

currency.label: Currency
currency.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

conversion_environment.label: Conversion Environment
conversion_environment.description: The environment this conversion was recorded on, e.g. APP or WEB. Sending the environment field requires an allowlist in your Google Ads account. Leave this field blank if your account has not been allowlisted.

merchant_id.label: Merchant Center ID
merchant_id.description: The ID of the Merchant Center account where the items are uploaded.

merchant_country_code.label: Merchant Center Feed Country Code
merchant_country_code.description: The ISO 3166 two-character region code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

merchant_language_code.label: Merchant Center Feed Language Code
merchant_language_code.description: The ISO 639-1 language code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

local_cost.label: Local Transaction Cost
local_cost.description: Sum of all transaction-level discounts, such as free shipping and coupon discounts for the whole cart.

product_id.label: Product ID
product_id.description: The ID of the item sold.

price.label: Price
price.description: Unit price excluding tax, shipping, and any transaction level discounts.

custom_variables.label: Custom Variables
custom_variables.description: The custom variables associated with this conversion. On the left-hand side, input the name of the custom variable as it appears in your Google Ads account. On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables.](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables)

ad_user_data_consent_state.label: Ad User Data Consent State
ad_user_data_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad user data.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_personalization_consent_state.label: Ad Personalization Consent State
ad_personalization_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

enable_batching.label: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

uploadCallConversion.title: Upload Call Conversion
uploadCallConversion.description: Upload an offline call conversion to the Google Ads API.

conversion_action.label: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

caller_id.label: Caller ID
caller_id.description: The caller ID from which this call was placed. Caller ID is expected to be in E.164 format with preceding + sign, e.g. "+16502531234".

call_timestamp.label: Call Timestamp
call_timestamp.description: The date time at which the call occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

conversion_timestamp.label: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

value.label: Value
value.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

currency.label: Currency
currency.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

custom_variables.label: Custom Variables
custom_variables.description: The custom variables associated with this conversion. On the left-hand side, input the name of the custom variable as it appears in your Google Ads account. On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables).

ad_user_data_consent_state.label: Ad User Data Consent State
ad_user_data_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad user data. For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_personalization_consent_state.label: Ad Personalization Consent State
ad_personalization_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

enable_batching.label: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

uploadConversionAdjustment.title: Upload Conversion Adjustment
uploadConversionAdjustment.description: Upload a conversion adjustment to the Google Ads API.

conversion_action.label: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

adjustment_type.label: Adjustment Type
adjustment_type.description: The adjustment type. See [Google’s documentation](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/rpc/v11/ConversionAdjustmentTypeEnum.ConversionAdjustmentType) for details on each type.

adjustment_timestamp.label: Adjustment Timestamp
adjustment_timestamp.description: The date time at which the adjustment occurred. Must be after the conversion timestamp. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". If no timestamp is provided, Segment will fall back on the current time.

order_id.label: Order ID
order_id.description: The order ID of the conversion to be adjusted. If the conversion was reported with an order ID specified, that order ID must be used as the identifier here.

gclid.label: GCLID
gclid.description: Google click ID associated with the original conversion for this adjustment. This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

conversion_timestamp.label: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the original conversion for this adjustment occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

restatement_value.label: Restatement Value
restatement_value.description: The restated conversion value. This is the value of the conversion after restatement. For example, to change the value of a conversion from 100 to 70, an adjusted value of 70 should be reported. Required for RESTATEMENT adjustments.

restatement_currency_code.label: Restatement Currency Code
restatement_currency_code.description: The currency of the restated value. If not provided, then the default currency from the conversion action is used, and if that is not set then the account currency is used. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code, e.g. USD or EUR.

email_address.label: Email Address
email_address.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

phone_country_code.label: Phone Number Country Code
phone_country_code.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

phone_number.label: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000

first_name.label: First Name
first_name.description: First name of the user who performed the conversion

last_name.label: Last Name
last_name.description: Last name of the user who performed the conversion

city.label: City
city.description: City of the user who performed the conversion.

state.label: State
state.description: State of the user who performed the conversion.

country.label: Country
country.description: 2-letter country code in ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 of the user who performed the conversion.

postal_code.label: Postal Code
postal_code.description: Postal code of the user who performed the conversion.

street_address.label: Street Address
street_address.description: Street address of the user who performed the conversion

user_agent.label: User Agent
user_agent.description: The user agent to enhance the original conversion. User agent can only be specified in enhancements with user identifiers. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device.

enable_batching.label: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

uploadConversionAdjustment2.title: Conversion Adjustment V2
uploadConversionAdjustment2.description: Send a conversion adjustment to the Google Ads API.

syncMode.label: How to sync records
syncMode.description: Define how the records from your destination will be synced.

conversion_action.label: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

adjustment_type.label: Adjustment Type
adjustment_type.description: The adjustment type. See [Google’s documentation](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/rpc/v11/ConversionAdjustmentTypeEnum.ConversionAdjustmentType) for details on each type.

adjustment_timestamp.label: Adjustment Timestamp
adjustment_timestamp.description: The date time at which the adjustment occurred. Must be after the conversion timestamp. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". If no timestamp is provided, Segment will fall back on the current time.

order_id.label: Order ID
order_id.description: The order ID of the conversion to be adjusted. If the conversion was reported with an order ID specified, that order ID must be used as the identifier here.

gclid.label: GCLID
gclid.description: Google click ID associated with the original conversion for this adjustment. This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

conversion_timestamp.label: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the original conversion for this adjustment occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

restatement_value.label: Restatement Value
restatement_value.description: The restated conversion value. This is the value of the conversion after restatement. For example, to change the value of a conversion from 100 to 70, an adjusted value of 70 should be reported. Required for RESTATEMENT adjustments.

restatement_currency_code.label: Restatement Currency Code
restatement_currency_code.description: The currency of the restated value. If not provided, then the default currency from the conversion action is used, and if that is not set then the account currency is used. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code, e.g. USD or EUR.

phone_country_code.label: Phone Number Country Code
phone_country_code.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

email_address.label: Email Address
email_address.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

phone_number.label: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000.

first_name.label: First Name
first_name.description: First name of the user who performed the conversion.

last_name.label: Last Name
last_name.description: Last name of the user who performed the conversion.

city.label: City
city.description: City of the user who performed the conversion.

state.label: State
state.description: State of the user who performed the conversion.

country.label: Country
country.description: 2-letter country code in ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 of the user who performed the conversion.

postal_code.label: Postal Code
postal_code.description: Postal code of the user who performed the conversion.

street_address.label: Street Address
street_address.description: Street address of the user who performed the conversion.

user_agent.label: User Agent
user_agent.description: The user agent to enhance the original conversion. User agent can only be specified in enhancements with user identifiers. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device.

enable_batching.label: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

ad_personalization_consent_state.label: Ad Personalization Consent State
ad_personalization_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

enable_batching.label: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

userList.title: Customer Match User List
userList.description: Sync users into a Google Customer Match User List.

syncMode.label: How to sync records
syncMode.description: Define how the records will be synced to Google

choices.label: Adds users to the connected Google Customer Match User List
Remove users from the connected Google Customer Match User List
Add and remove users in the connected Google Customer Match User List

retlOnMappingSave.label: Connect to a Google Customer Match User List
retlOnMappingSave.description: When saving this mapping, we will create a list in Google using the fields you provide.

list_id.label: Existing List ID
list_id.description: The ID of an existing Google list that you would like to sync users to. If you provide this, we will not create a new list.

list_name.label: List Name
list_name.description: The name of the Google list that you would like to create.

app_id.label: App ID
app_id.description: A string that uniquely identifies a mobile application from which the data was collected. Required if external ID type is mobile advertising ID

id.label: ID
id.description: The ID of the Google Customer Match User list that users will be synced to.

name.label: List Name
name.description: The name of the Google Customer Match User list that users will be synced to.

phone_country_code.label: Phone Number Country Code
phone_country_code.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

country_code.label: Address Country Code
country_code.description: 2-letter country code in ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 of the user's address

postal_code.label: Postal Code
postal_code.description: Postal code of the user's address.

crm_id.label: CRM ID
crm_id.description: Advertiser-assigned user ID for Customer Match upload. Required if external ID type is CRM ID.

mobile_advertising_id.label: Mobile Advertising ID
mobile_advertising_id.description: Mobile device ID (advertising ID/IDFA). Required if external ID type is mobile advertising ID.

ad_user_data_consent_state.label: Ad User Data Consent State
ad_user_data_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad user data.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_personalization_consent_state.label: Ad Personalization Consent State
ad_personalization_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

external_audience_id.label: External Audience ID
external_audience_id.description: The ID of the List that users will be synced to.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: The number of records to send in each batch.

event_name.label: Event Name
event_name.description: The name of the current Segment event.

sessionAttributesEncoded.title: Session Attributes Encoded Plugin
sessionAttributesEncoded.description: Enriches Segment payloads with Session Attributes Encoded values from the page URL.
sessionAttributesEncoded.defaultSubscription: type = "track" or type = "identify" or type = "page" or type = "group" or type = "alias"

presets.name: Session Attributes Encoded Plugin
presets.subscribe: type = "track" or type = "identify" or type = "group" or type = "page" or type = "alias"
